headline: HPE, Oracle, and Adobe Earnings Coming Next Week
summary: It’s a packed week for tech earnings, and three companies are coming in with very different stories to tell. HPE: The Juniper Bet Gets Its First Real Test Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) reports Monday after the market closes, and the headline question is simple: is the Juniper Networks acquisition actually working? HPE’s most recent quarter ... HPE, Oracle, and Adobe Earnings Coming Next Week

headline: Adobe: The Problems Could Be Bigger Than We Think (Rating Downgrade)
summary: Adobe is now priced at a deep discount, trading at just over 12x forward earnings, reflecting severe market pessimism. See why ADBE stock is downgraded to hold.

headline: Michael Burry Rumored Adobe Stake Sparks Fresh Look At Valuation Risks
summary: Rumors suggest Michael Burry has taken a new position in NasdaqGS:ADBE, going against prevailing pessimism around the stock. The move comes after a sharp decline in Adobe's share price, with some investors viewing current levels as a discount. Speculation is growing that Burry's interest could act as a contrarian signal for sentiment toward Adobe's long term prospects. Adobe, listed on NasdaqGS:ADBE, is best known for its Creative Cloud suite and a broad set of tools used by designers,...

headline: Previewing The 2026 Q1 Earnings Season: A Closer Look
summary: The 2026 Q1 earnings season will take the spotlight when the big banks report their results in about four weeks, but we will already have seen fiscal February-quarter results from almost two dozen companies by then, including Oracle and Adobe.

headline: Oracle earnings, February CPI, housing data: What to Watch
summary: Market Domination Overtime Host Jared Blikre previews several of the biggest stories to come throughout next week, including earnings results from Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), Kohl's (KSS), Oracle (ORCL), and Adobe (ADBE); February's Consumer Price Index survey (CPI) results; and the latest readings on NFIB Optimism Index and US existing home sales. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.
